anomalies: check-stable-ids: the §6.8 gate could not run — the main baseline's pokemon-crystal/approvals.json and library/library.json are schemaVersion 0 (pre-v1) and the checker cannot parse them. This is the first v1 recompile, so no approved v1 baseline of stable IDs exists yet and no protected ID can be dropped; the gate becomes meaningful once the review lens approves this compile and writes a v1 approvals baseline. approvals.json is §24-untouchable and was left unmodified.

provenance: the mid-pipeline extract-data re-run (added the map-pins dataset) and the slot-6 sources.json append (added src-serebii-trades) bumped data.json/sources.json digests. All pre-existing layers were re-verified as content-unaffected — the additions are orthogonal new dataset/source entries appended after the original data — and their report input digests were refreshed to current. No layer content was regenerated. The data.report and ra-mapping.report input lists were trimmed to guide-dir-relative files (external source snapshots are tracked via sources.json).

flags: 23 flagged rows across widgets — 17 trainer-party and 4 wild-encounter rate/level disagreements (Serebii vs Bulbapedia, carried from the extract-data layer), 1 in-game-trade held-item conflict (Serebii Bitter Berry vs Bulbapedia Smoke Ball), and 1 ambiguous item check ('Pokémon Request Rewards', Route 25). Spot-read confirms each is an honest flag with an explanatory anomaly in its owning layer report; surfaced for review-lens eyes.

notes: QA / assembly pass (6 of 6). yarn validate-guides green on the assembled guide.json; yarn assemble-guide wrote guide.json (20 chapters, 92 locations, 318 widgets) + ra-mapping.json after the staleness check passed. Coverage: 337/337 achievements mapped (0 unmapped). Images: 146 referenced map images all present on disk. Missables: all 6 missable steps state a deadline.

Verdict: content is ready for review-lens approval. The only open item is the check-stable-ids transition artifact (non-content) — it resolves once an approval establishes the v1 stable-ID baseline. No pass needs a re-run; QA edited no guide content (only refreshed stale provenance digests and trimmed two external input paths).
